event: Our Lady in a Garland of Roses
symbols: garland of roses — the rosary, Mary's beauty, and the mystical rose of paradise
red roses — the blood of Christ and the sorrow of the Mater Dolorosa
white roses — purity and the immaculate nature of the Virgin
pink roses — grace and the gentleness of divine love
stars around her head — the twelve stars of the woman in Revelation 12
blue mantle — heavenly grace enveloping the Mother of God
hands crossed on chest — acceptance, humility, and the posture of the Annunciation
downcast eyes — the humility of the handmaid of the Lord
description: A half-length portrait of the Virgin Mary surrounded by a large oval garland of roses in various colors — red, white, pink, and blush. She wears a red dress beneath a blue mantle and a golden-edged veil. Her hands are crossed over her chest in a gesture of devotion. Her eyes are modestly downcast. Small star-like points of light circle her head like a crown. The background within the garland is dark, making the figure and flowers luminous by contrast.
theological_description: Sassoferrato's Our Lady in a Garland of Roses presents the Virgin Mary as the 'Mystical Rose' of Catholic devotion — a title drawn from the Litany of Loreto. The floral wreath surrounding her connects to the Song of Solomon's imagery: 'I am a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys' (Song of Solomon 2:1), which Marian theology has applied to the Blessed Virgin. The stars around her head directly evoke Revelation 12:1 — 'a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon under her feet, and on her head a crown of twelve stars.' Her hands crossed on her chest recall the moment of the Annunciation when Mary responded to Gabriel: 'Behold, I am the servant of the Lord; let it be to me according to your word' (Luke 1:38). The roses themselves carry rich symbolism — red for the sorrows of the Passion through which a sword pierced her own soul (Luke 2:35), white for her immaculate purity, and pink for the grace with which she was filled. Isaiah's prophecy resonates: 'The desert shall rejoice and blossom like the rose... it shall blossom abundantly and rejoice with joy and singing' (Isaiah 35:1-2). Mary is the garden enclosed (Song of Solomon 4:12), the flowering of Israel's long hope.

scripture_refs.reason: The woman with a crown of twelve stars — the star-crown around Mary's head directly references this vision
Mary's fiat — her hands crossed on her chest recall the posture of humble acceptance
The rose of Sharon — the floral wreath surrounding Mary draws on this Song of Solomon imagery
The desert blossoming like a rose — the abundance of roses signifying the flowering of salvation
The sword piercing Mary's soul — the red roses symbolizing her sorrows through the Passion

_meta.title: Our Lady in a garland of roses
